narrative: Jindal Stainless is again the overall leader, ranking 1st on Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3, physical-output intensity and Scope 1+2 turnover intensity. Its FY2024 Scope 3 disclosure begins to make it more comparable, and it reports 3.35 MtCO2e Scope 3 and 2.15 tCO2e/tcs physical intensity. SAIL is comparatively stronger in Scope 3, ranking 2nd on absolute Scope 3 and 1st on Scope 3 turnover intensity, but remains weaker on Scope 1+2 operational metrics. Tata is the laggard for Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3 and physical-output intensity in FY2024, with 56 MtCO2e Scope 1, 7 MtCO2e Scope 2, 15 MtCO2e Scope 3 and 3.1 tCO2e/tcs. The deterioration in Tata’s ranking reflects the transition from FY2023 consolidated data to standardised standalone comparison, BF-based emission intensity and the absence of a near-term intensity reduction visible in the table. Jindal’s initiatives—floating solar, rooftop solar, chrome pelletisation, waste-heat recovery and annealing bypass—help explain its lower carbon profile.
